subject: [RFC PATCH 30/45] mm: page_alloc: drive slab shrink from SPB anti-fragmentation pressure

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new mechanism to drive slab shrink from SPB anti-fragmentation pressure, which prevents the system from running out of memory due to excessive slab growth. The problem it solves is that GFP_KERNEL slab callers have no fallback and can lead to sustained slab growth, draining the tainted pool and causing fragmentation. To address this, the patch adds a parallel slab-shrink mechanism that mirrors the existing evacuation infrastructure, allowing for the consolidation of movable pages out of tainted SPBs and freeing up pageblocks for re-coalescing. The new mechanism is triggered by three sites: __rmqueue_smallest, __rmqueue_claim, and end of spb_evacuate_for_order. This approach aims to prevent the system from plateauing at a high tainted-SPB count and instead maintain equilibrium at a lower level.
